title: Hieracium racemosum-levigatum
intro: Hieracium racemosum-levigatum, also known as levigated hawkweed, is a subspecies of the Hieracium racemosum plant, which belongs to the sunflower family (Asteraceae) and is characterized by its dandelion-like appearance and presence of milky sap. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including treating digestive issues, reducing inflammation, and supporting immune function. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium racemosum-levigatum include flavonoids, sesquiterpenes, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. In herbal medicine, levigated hawkweed is typically prepared as a tincture, tea, or infusion, and is often combined with other herbs to support overall well-being and promote liver health.
taxonomy: Hieracium racemosum-levigatum is a subspecies of Hieracium racemosum. It belongs to the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. As a member of this family, it is closely related to other plants such as daisies and thistles. Hieracium racemosum-levigatum is further classified as belonging to the genus Hieracium, which comprises hawkweeds. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics, including its leaf shape and flower arrangement.
